content: p PALO, Iowa There are two restaurants in Palo, not counting the chicken wings and pizza sold at the only gas station in town.  p   p All three establishments, including the gas station, stand on the same half-mile stretch of First Street, an artery that divides the marshy floodplain of the Cedar River to the east from hundreds of acres of cornfields on the west.  p   p During historic flooding in 2008, the Cedar River surged 10 feet above its previous record, cresting at 31 feet and wiping out homes and businesses well outside the floodplain.  p  p  a href    full article  a   p   p  a href

metadata.author: Anika Jane Beamer, Inside Climate News
